title: PrincipleResolutionRecord
description: Immutable record of a principle arbitration decision produced by AEP-MEM-003. Every principle conflict is logged — the system never silently chooses. The losing principle receives the applied_disposition lifecycle transition but is preserved as a historical record. The resolution creates a first-class causal link between the two principles and records both the arbitration reason (conflict_type) and the lifecycle outcome (applied_disposition) as separate fields.

resolution_id.description: Content-addressed identifier derived from principle_a, principle_b, conflict_type, and winner. Deterministic and deduplication-safe.

principle_a.description: principle_id of the first participant in the conflict.

principle_b.description: principle_id of the second participant in the conflict.

conflict_type.description: Classification of the conflict that required arbitration. Records why the arbitration engine reached a decision. TAXONOMY_OVERRIDE: the two principles sit at different taxonomy hierarchy levels; the higher-authority level wins unconditionally. SPECIFICITY_COLLISION: the two principles sit at the same taxonomy level but cover different scope widths; the narrower (more specific) scope wins. AGGREGATION_LIMIT: no single pairwise conflict, but the combined tolerance contributions of multiple active principles would breach the global invariant envelope; detected by aggregateEnvelopeCheck(), not recorded as a pairwise resolution.

winner.description: principle_id of the principle whose constraint prevails. Remains ACTIVE in the registry.

loser.description: principle_id of the principle that was displaced. Receives the applied_disposition lifecycle transition. The principle entry itself is preserved as an immutable historical record.

applied_disposition.description: Lifecycle disposition applied to the losing principle as a result of this arbitration event. Records what happened to the loser, independently of why the arbitration was triggered (conflict_type). SUBORDINATED: the loser is temporarily suppressed and may return to ACTIVE. SUPERSEDED: the loser is permanently replaced. DEPRECATED: the loser is frozen for legacy replay only. RETIRED: the loser is removed from all future consideration. The default for pairwise arbitration conflicts (TAXONOMY_OVERRIDE, SPECIFICITY_COLLISION) is SUBORDINATED.

priority_delta.description: Human-readable explanation of why the winner prevailed. For TAXONOMY_OVERRIDE: records the class and rank of each participant (e.g. 'LEVEL_0_DETERMINISM_INTEGRITY (rank 0) > LEVEL_3_BEHAVIORAL_OPTIMIZATION (rank 3)'). For SPECIFICITY_COLLISION: records the scope descriptor and specificity score of each participant.

simulation_verified.description: True when simulation was used to confirm the resolution outcome. Provides additional assurance beyond structural rule application.
